evals.prompt: TARA provides a traffic forecast of 800 AADT with 20% heavy vehicles growing at 4% annually for a trunk road in Uganda. The road is being upgraded from gravel to paved standard with a 20-year design period. Calculate the design ESA using OpenEng skills and identify the traffic class for pavement design.
evals.expected_output: Maps TARA traffic to Skill #6 for growth application (including 10-20% generated traffic for gravel-to-paved), then calculates cumulative ESA via Skill #5 using MoWT methodology. Design year AADT approximately 1,750-2,000 vpd (with generated traffic). Cumulative ESA approximately 5-8 million ESA. Traffic class T5-T6 per MoWT classification.

assertions.text: Correctly maps TARA traffic data to Skill #6 (Traffic Forecasting) as the first step, applying the 4% growth rate
Includes generated traffic factor (10-20%) from Skill #6 for gravel-to-paved surface upgrade
Calculates cumulative ESA via Skill #5 using AADT, VDF, growth rate, and 20-year design period
Identifies MoWT traffic class (T4-T6 range) appropriate for the calculated ESA magnitude
Notes that Skill #5 produces cumulative ESA while TARA may work with annual values, flagging the distinction

evals.prompt: Road condition surveys on a 30 km Uganda trunk road show IRI = 9.5 m/km and VCI = 25. The road needs economic justification for rehabilitation funding from the World Bank. Format the condition data for TARA economic analysis and identify the appropriate maintenance intervention using OpenEng skills.
evals.expected_output: Classifies condition as Very Poor (IRI >8 threshold) via Skill #56. Identifies rehabilitation trigger via Skill #57 (IRI exceeds 6 m/km threshold). Formats before/after IRI trajectories for TARA (current IRI 9.5 to post-rehabilitation ~2.5 m/km). Notes WB discount rate (6%) for economic analysis. Identifies cost pipeline inputs needed from Skills #50-53.

assertions.text: Correctly classifies IRI 9.5 m/km as Very Poor and VCI 25 as Poor/Very Poor via Skill #56
Identifies rehabilitation as the intervention via Skill #57 based on IRI exceeding the rehabilitation trigger threshold
Formats with-project and without-project IRI trajectories for TARA before/after comparison
Specifies World Bank discount rate (6% or project-specific) rather than GoU 12% for WB-funded project
Identifies that cost data from Skill #50 is needed to complete the TARA cost pipeline

evals.prompt: A district road in rural Uganda has no traffic counts and no condition survey data. A TARA screening analysis is needed to determine if the road warrants investment. What assumptions should be used and what data should be collected as priority?
evals.expected_output: Identifies data gaps for both traffic and condition pipelines. Proposes reasonable defaults: AADT from road class defaults (Skill #2 module, 50-200 vpd for district road), assumed IRI 10-14 m/km for unmaintained road. Flags all assumptions clearly. Notes that BCR likely <1.0 at 12% for low-traffic district road. Recommends non-economic justification. Specifies priority data collection: 7-day traffic count first, then visual condition survey.

assertions.text: Identifies both traffic and condition data gaps as the primary challenge
Proposes class-based AADT estimates from Skill #2 Uganda module defaults as reasonable traffic assumption
Proposes assumed IRI based on road age/surface type as condition estimate, flagging clearly as assumption
Flags that BCR is likely below 1.0 at 12% discount rate for low-traffic district road and recommends non-economic justification
Recommends specific priority data collection (traffic count and condition survey) before full appraisal
